steps.title: Read the center and radius
steps.content: The equation $(x+3)^{2}+(y-18)^{2}=169$ is in standard circle form $(x-h)^{2}+(y-k)^{2}=r^{2}$, giving center $(-3,18)$ and radius $r=\sqrt{169}=13$.

steps.title: Bound the x-coordinate of any point on the circle
steps.content: For a point $(a,b)$ on the circle, $a$ must lie within one radius of the center's $x$-coordinate: $-3-13 \le a \le -3+13$, i.e. $-16 \le a \le 10$.

steps.title: Test each choice against the interval
steps.content:
Compare each value of $a$ to $[-16,10]$:
  • A) $a=-17$: $-17\lt -16$, outside the interval — eliminate.
  • B) $a=-12$: $-16 \le -12 \le 10$, inside the interval — possible.
  • C) $a=13$: $13>10$, outside the interval — eliminate.
  • D) $a=18$: $18>10$, outside the interval — eliminate.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
